If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.35-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y (Not able to reproduce consistently) **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @Leeckhart **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773152768885049) ## Action Performed: **Scenario : 1** 1. Admin Enables Require Attendees in Rules 2. Admin goes to Workspace Settings > Categories 3. Opens the category Business Meals (Attendees) 4. Goes to Category rules > Require fields 5. Saves / exits the category settings 6. Re-opens the same category to confirm the rule. **Scenario:2** 1. Go to Settings > Workspaces > [Workspace Name] > Categories 2. Select a category imported from NetSuite (e.g., "Business Meals (Attendees)") 3. Enable Category Rules to "Require Attendees" and "Require Description." 4. Save the settings and confirm they are active 5. Perform a manual or automated sync with NetSuite via Settings > Workspaces > [Workspace Name] > Connections > NetSuite > Sync Now 6. Return to the Categories settings for that specific category ## Expected Result: “Attendees” should remain enabled and be visible under Category rules > Require fields for that category. ## Actual Result: The setting does not persist: after saving and reopening the category, “Attendees” is not enabled and does not appear under Require fields (only “Description” is shown). ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/83b7b254-ad76-4c0f-83d6-574353fb8b08 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Full story : “Get paid back from my employer” onboarding flow does not work for BYOC users

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.description: Reproducible steps needed

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Reproduction blocked) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @danielrvidal **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): #[Convert](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07HPDRELLD/p1773220736936909?thread_ts=1773201569.695189&cid=C07HPDRELLD) ## Action Performed: ## Description: During onboarding, the “Get paid back from my employer” option does not work well for BYOC (Bring Your Own Company) users who have not yet been invited to their company’s workspace. In observed Track sessions, users are signing up for Expensify because their employer uses it, but they have not yet received a workspace invitation. When these users select “Get paid back from my employer,” the flow does not properly accommodate their situation. Many of these users are simply trying to track expenses or prepare expenses before they are invited, especially if they have a corporate card or are not reimbursed directly through Expensify. Currently, the flow only allows them to skip, instead of allowing them to create a workspace or proceed with expense tracking. ## Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a new Expensify account without being invited to a workspace 2. During onboarding, select “Get paid back from my employer.” 3. Continue through the onboarding flow ## Expected Result: The onboarding flow should support BYOC users who have not yet been invited. Users should have the option to: Create a workspace, or Continue with “Track and budget expenses” if reimbursement via Expensify is not applicable yet. The flow should not force users to skip. ## Actual Result: The “Get paid back from my employer” flow does not properly support users who have not yet been invited to a workspace. Users are effectively forced to skip the step ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The link for the full stork is in slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07HPDRELLD/p1773220736936909?thread_ts=1773201569.695189&cid=C07HPDRELLD) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: New Feature: Improve approval workflow readability with user pill component and tooltip for "+X more" and limit attendee display two rows with avatar + name and truncate remaining with “+X more"

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** **Reproducible in production?:** **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @shawnborton **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Convert](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07HPDRELLD/p1773231200641309) ## Action Performed: ## Description: Approval workflow rows can become difficult to scan when many users are listed. Currently, the names appear as plain text, which makes it harder to quickly identify users and visually parse the workflow. To improve readability, we should introduce a small “user pill” component that includes the user’s avatar alongside their name. This will make the list easier to scan and help users quickly recognize approvers. Additionally, when workflows contain more users than can be displayed, the “+X more” indicator should include a tooltip showing the additional users. Selecting the “+X more” element should continue to behave as it currently does by opening the full Right-Hand Panel (RHP) with the complete workflow details. ## Steps to Reproduce for improving approval workflow readability: 1. Navigate to Workspace > Workflows 2. Open an approval workflow with multiple users listed 3. Observe the workflow rows containing several approvers ## Expected Result: Approvers are displayed using a user pill component that includes: Small avatar User name This makes workflow rows easier to visually scan. The “+X more” indicator includes a tooltip listing additional users. Clicking or tapping “+X more” opens the Right-Hand Panel (RHP) with the full workflow, as it currently does. ## Actual Result: Approvers are displayed as plain text, which can be difficult to scan when many users are listed. ## Steps to Reproduce for limiting attendee display two rows with avatar + name and truncate remaining with “+X more": ## Action Performed: 1. Create an expense 2. Add many attendees to the expense 3. View the attendee list in the expense details. ## Expected Result: Attendees are displayed using avatar + name components. The attendee list shows a maximum of two rows. If additional attendees exist beyond two rows: The list is truncated. A “+X more” indicator appears showing the number of remaining attendees. Selecting “+X more” opens the full attendee list (e.g., RHP). ## Actual Result: Attendees are displayed as plain text, ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @nyomanjyotisa

  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022031864010605736142
  • Upwork Job ID: 2031864010605736142
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-03-11
